stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML technologies, specifically text-generation pipelines.
Key associated technologies include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The model belongs to the Llama family, indicating relevance within the current large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A zero-day exploit has been discovered that bypasses default BitLocker protections in Windows 11.
The specific mechanism of the exploit is currently unknown, and Microsoft is actively investigating.
This represents a significant security vulnerability affecting a core Windows feature.

stories.title: XFP: Quality-Targeted Adaptive Codebook Quantization with Sparse Outlier Separation for LLM Inference
stories.author: Thomas Witt
bullet_points: Introduces XFP, a novel dynamic weight quantizer specifically designed for efficient LLM inference.
Inverts traditional quantization workflows by allowing operators to set reconstruction quality floors directly, eliminating manual calibration and bit-width selection.
Achieves efficiency through automatic determination of codebook size and utilizes sparse outlier separation for weight matrix decomposition.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, offering a faster execution experience for users on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI is integrating Codex, its code-writing AI tool, into the ChatGPT mobile application.
This move is part of OpenAI's effort to compete with rivals like Anthropic's Claude Code.
The update allows users to access Codex's desktop functionality (code writing, app use) via their phone.

stories.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: An audit in Ontario revealed significant errors in AI-powered doctor notetakers.
Common mistakes include the generation of fabricated therapy referrals and incorrect prescriptions.
The story highlights critical reliability and accuracy concerns regarding AI adoption in healthcare.

stories.title: Do Coding Agents Understand Least-Privilege Authorization?
stories.author: Mengkang Hu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical security need for least-privilege authorization as coding AI agents gain broader system access.
It introduces 'permission-boundary inference,' a method for models to automatically map a task to a precise file-level read/write/execute policy.
The research investigates whether current large language models can autonomously infer the necessary security boundaries for safe agent deployment.

stories.title: Emotion-Attended Stateful Memory (EASM):The Architecture for Hyper-Personalization at Scale
stories.author: Vansh Gupta
bullet_points: Proposes Emotion-Attended Stateful Memory (EASM) to overcome the stateless nature of current LLMs.
EASM enables hyper-personalization by dynamically constructing user context using long-term history and emotional signals.
Addresses a key limitation in current NLP systems where personalization is limited despite RAG and fine-tuning.

stories.title: XFP: Quality-Targeted Adaptive Codebook Quantization with Sparse Outlier Separation for LLM Inference
stories.author: Thomas Witt
bullet_points: Introduces XFP, a novel, quality-targeted adaptive codebook quantizer specifically designed for LLM inference.
It inverts the standard workflow by allowing operators to set reconstruction quality floors, enabling automatic determination of codebook size and outlier budget.
The method achieves quantization without relying on traditional calibration data or manual bit-width selection, decomposing weights into sparse residuals.

stories.title: Video2GUI: Synthesizing Large-Scale Interaction Trajectories for Generalized GUI Agent Pretraining
stories.author: Hao Tian
bullet_points: Addresses the critical data scarcity issue in training generalized GUI agents.
Introduces Video2GUI, a fully automated framework to extract large-scale, grounded GUI interaction trajectories from video.
Aims to overcome limitations of existing datasets that rely on costly manual annotations and are domain-specific.

stories.title: Mechanical Enforcement for LLM Governance:Evidence of Governance-Task Decoupling in Financial Decision Systems
stories.author: Carlos Martí-González
bullet_points: Addresses a critical governance failure in LLMs used in regulated financial workflows: the model interpreting its own compliance policies.
Introduces five novel governance metrics designed to quantify policy compliance at the decision rationale level, moving beyond simple task accuracy.
Highlights the need for 'mechanical enforcement' to ensure LLM outputs are truly compliant and auditable in high-stakes environments.

stories.title: Agentifying Patient Dynamics within LLMs through Interacting with Clinical World Model
stories.author: Hongyuan Zha
bullet_points: Introduces SepsisAgent, an LLM agent designed to assist in critical care decision-making for sepsis management.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs by integrating a learned Clinical World Model to simulate patient physiological responses.
Focuses on grounding LLM reasoning in action-conditioned patient dynamics for sequential treatment recommendations.

stories.title: First public macOS kernel memory corruption exploit on Apple M5
stories.author: quadrige
bullet_points: A public exploit targeting macOS kernel memory corruption has been discovered.
The vulnerability specifically affects Apple M5 chips/hardware.
This represents a significant security finding in the Apple ecosystem.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its compatibility to include the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is adding virtual writing capabilities to its Meta Ray-Ban Display smart glasses.
Users can now write messages using only hand gestures.
The feature is integrated across major platforms like WhatsApp, Messenger, and native Android/iOS messaging.

stories.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft began offering access to Anthropic's Claude Code to its developers in December.
The initial rollout aimed to encourage non-coders (like PMs and designers) to experiment with coding.
The story indicates that Microsoft is now starting to cancel these Claude Code licenses, suggesting a shift in strategy or evaluation.

stories.title: Energy supplier abandons Lake Tahoe residents to serve data centers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Local residents in Lake Tahoe are facing energy competition from large data centers.
An energy supplier is prioritizing service to data centers over the needs of the town's 49,000 residents.
The issue highlights the growing strain that data center energy demands place on local infrastructure.

stories.title: Cisco announces record revenue and 4,000 layoffs in the same day
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Cisco reported record revenue alongside a significant announcement of 4,000 layoffs.
The company's CFO publicly stated that the layoffs are not driven by cost-cutting measures.
The news highlights a tension between strong financial performance and workforce reduction in the enterprise tech sector.

stories.title: GPart: End-to-End Isometric Fine-Tuning via Global Parameter Partitioning
stories.author: Neo Christopher Chung
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical limitation in Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), the dominant PEFT method for LLMs.
LoRA's bilinear structure causes the mapping from trainable parameters to weight updates to be non-distance-preserving, distorting optimization.
The proposed method, GPart, aims to achieve end-to-end isometric fine-tuning by using Global Parameter Partitioning.

stories.title: A Heterogeneous Temporal Memory Governance Framework for Long-Term LLM Persona Consistency
stories.author: Lin Hujite
bullet_points: Addresses key LLM limitations like persona drift and fact loss during long-term interactions.
Introduces ARPM, an external temporal memory governance framework for dialogue.
ARPM separates static knowledge from dynamic dialogue experience, utilizing advanced retrieval techniques (vector, BM25, RRF fusion).

stories.title: GPart: End-to-End Isometric Fine-Tuning via Global Parameter Partitioning
stories.author: Neo Christopher Chung
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical limitation in Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), the dominant PEFT method for LLMs, where its bilinear structure distorts the optimization landscape.
The research proposes 'GPart,' an end-to-end isometric fine-tuning approach utilizing Global Parameter Partitioning.
The work aims to overcome issues in existing methods (like Uni-LoRA) where the subsequent bilinear LoRA mapping breaks end-to-end consistency.

stories.title: GenAI for Energy-Efficient and Interference-Aware Compressed Sensing of GNSS Signals on a Google Edge TPU
stories.author: Alexander Rügamer
bullet_points: Proposes using Generative AI (GenAI) for real-time, on-device classification of GNSS jamming and spoofing attacks.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional methods by performing data compression and classification directly at the hardware receiver (Google Edge TPU).
Offers a solution to reduce the need for costly, complex data transmission to cloud-based systems for interference detection.

stories.title: GFMate: Empowering Graph Foundation Models with Test-time Prompt Tuning
stories.author: Zi Huang
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'GFMate' to enhance Graph Foundation Models (GFMs) using test-time prompt tuning.
Existing graph prompt tuning methods often embed source-domain information directly into prompts or during pre-training.
GFMate aims to address the limitation of prompt entanglement by improving GFM performance via auxiliary prompts.

stories.title: Graphs of Research: Citation Evolution Graphs as Supervision for Research Idea Generation
stories.author: Hui Xiong
bullet_points: Introduces 'Graphs of Research' (GoR), a novel method to improve research idea generation using LLMs.
GoR addresses a limitation in current LLM methods by incorporating the structural relationships (2-hop connections) between cited literature.
The technique utilizes supervised fine-tuning to leverage citation evolution graphs as supervision for automated scientific discovery.

stories.title: Streaming Speech-to-Text Translation with a SpeechLLM
stories.author: Rogier C. van Dalen
bullet_points: The research proposes a SpeechLLM to combine speech recognition and text translation into a single model.
The primary advantage is leveraging paralinguistic information and reducing errors from cascaded systems.
A key limitation addressed is the lack of real-time streaming capability in existing SpeechLLM systems.

stories.title: Persian MusicGen: A Large-Scale Dataset and Culturally-Aware Generative Model for Persian Music
stories.author: Mahdieh Soleymani Baghshah
bullet_points: Introduces 'Persian MusicGen,' a generative model specifically designed for Persian music.
Addresses the limitations of Western-centric music generation models by focusing on unique Persian musical elements (Dastgah, tonalities).
Presents a novel, large-scale dataset of over 900 hours of high-quality Persian music across various sub-genres.

stories.title: Non-linear Interventions on Large Language Models
stories.author: Sangwoo Kim
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM interpretability methods, which are primarily confined to linear interventions.
It introduces a general formulation of intervention capable of analyzing features encoded along non-linear manifolds within LLMs.
This work aims to extend the understanding of LLM internal representations beyond the constraints of the Linear Representation Hypothesis.

stories.title: IntentVLA: Short-Horizon Intent Modeling for Aliased Robot Manipulation
stories.author: Kai Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of multimodal robot imitation data where similar observations can lead to different actions due to underlying short-horizon intents.
Identifies that existing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies struggle with partial observability, causing intent resampling and execution instability.
Introduces 'IntentVLA' as a method to model short-horizon intents to improve the robustness and consistency of robot manipulation policies.

stories.title: Tesla Wall Connector bootloader bypasses the firmware downgrade ratchet
stories.author: p_stuart82
bullet_points: A vulnerability was discovered in the Tesla Wall Connector bootloader.
The bypass allows for circumventing the firmware downgrade protection mechanism.
This represents a security flaw in a widely used EV charging hardware component.

stories.title: Old PC vs New AI: Can a 2015 Desktop Actually Run Gemma 4? (2B vs 4B Benchmark)
stories.author: gramli
bullet_points: The article explores the feasibility of running modern AI models (like Gemma 4) on older, legacy hardware (a 2015 desktop).
It focuses on benchmarking smaller, optimized models (2B vs 4B) to test local inference capabilities.
The topic is relevant to the growing trend of edge AI and local LLM deployment, despite the hardware limitations.

stories.title: I Ran SERP Feature Detection on 8 Nigerian Creator Queries. Every Single One Had an AI Overview.
stories.author: dannwaneri
bullet_points: The article details the implementation of a SERP feature detection module for an SEO agent.
The author tested this module on queries targeting Nigerian creators.
The key finding is the pervasive presence of AI Overviews across all tested queries.

stories.title: Gemma 4 26B on v6e-4 Turbo-Stable Benchmark
stories.author: xbill
bullet_points: The Gemma 4 MoE stack has reached its definitive production state.
The evaluation was conducted on the TPU v6e-4 hardware.
The benchmark used for testing was the Turbo-Stable Benchmark.
